author: garyrong

author: lu_pinto
content:
> a) relocate the account-creation state gas into the child frame
I've already given my opinion as well, basically:

CALL and CREATE have different behaviours right now regarding accessing the said account (for creation or not), shouldn't we distinguish both cases? I think we should charge the create at the same time we are trying to load the account. Basically my thinking is what's the rationale for changing the current behaviour?

- CALL: we need to get the code for the account for what to run in the frame. Doesn't make sense to charge the child for its account creation (this will decrease the gas stipend in the child)
- CREATE: don't need the account access until we are in the initcode frame. Which is fine cause initcode frames have boundless gas. It actually matters as CREATE returns 0 if unsuccessful. If you move the OOG check before getting into CREATE you are essentially OOGing the caller so this changes behaviour.
